children.label: I
children.text: to improve public safety and reduce vehicle-caused wildlife mortality while maintaining habitat connectivity; and

children.label: II
children.text: to mitigate the damage to wildlife, aquatic organism passage, habitat, and ecosystem connectivity, including the costs of constructing, maintaining, replacing, or removing culverts and bridges, as appropriate;

children.label: vi

children.label: B
children.text: capital, operations, and maintenance of transit facilities;

children.label: C
children.text: any transportation project eligible for assistance under this title that is on a public road within or adjacent to, or that provides access to, Federal lands open to the public; and

children.label: D
children.text: not more $20,000,000 of the amounts made available per fiscal year to carry out this section for activities eligible under subparagraph (A)(iv)(I).1So in original.1

children.text: a State (including a political subdivision of a State); or

children.label: B

children.heading: .—Administration
children.label: 3 .—Administration
children.text: All appropriations for the construction and improvement of Federal lands transportation facilities shall be administered in conformity with regulations and agreements jointly approved by the Secretary and the Secretary of the appropriate Federal land managing agency.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The cooperation of States, counties, or other local subdivisions may be accepted in construction and improvement.

children.heading: .—Funds received
children.label: B .—Funds received
children.text: Any funds received from a State, county, or local subdivision shall be credited to appropriations available for the class of Federal lands transportation facilities to which the funds were contributed.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), construction of each project shall be performed by contract awarded by competitive bidding.

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: B .—Exception
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply if the Secretary or the Secretary of the appropriate Federal land management agency affirmatively finds that, under the circumstances relating to the project, a different method is in the public interest.

children.label: A
children.text: the use of locally adapted native plant materials; and

children.label: B
children.text: designs that minimize runoff and heat generation.

children.label: A
children.text: in consultation with the Secretaries of the applicable Federal land management agencies; and

children.label: i
children.text: the National Park Service;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Forest Service;

children.label: iii
children.text: the United States Fish and Wildlife Service;

children.label: iv
children.text: the Corps of Engineers;

children.label: v
children.text: the Bureau of Land Management;

children.label: vi
children.text: the Bureau of Reclamation; and

children.label: vii
children.text: independent Federal agencies with natural resource and land management responsibilities.

children.heading: .—Requirements
children.label: A .—Requirements
children.text: Each application submitted by a Federal land management agency shall include proposed programs at various potential funding levels, as defined by the Secretary following collaborative discussions with applicable Federal land management agencies.

children.label: I
children.text: a state of good repair of transportation facilities;

children.label: II
children.text: a reduction of bridge deficiencies; and

children.label: III
children.text: an improvement of safety;

children.label: ii
children.text: high-use Federal recreational sites or Federal economic generators; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the resource and asset management goals of the Secretary of the respective Federal land management agency.

children.heading: .—Permissive contents
children.label: C .—Permissive contents
children.text: Applications may include proposed programs the duration of which extend over a multiple-year period to support long-term transportation planning and resource management initiatives.

children.heading: National Federal Lands Transportation Facility Inventory.—
children.label: c National Federal Lands Transportation Facility Inventory.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretaries of the appropriate Federal land management agencies, in cooperation with the Secretary, shall maintain a comprehensive national inventory of public Federal lands transportation facilities.

children.label: A
children.text: provide access to high-use Federal recreation sites or Federal economic generators, as determined by the Secretary in coordination with the respective Secretaries of the appropriate Federal land management agencies; and

children.label: i
children.text: The National Park Service.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Forest Service.

children.label: iii
children.text: The United States Fish and Wildlife Service.

children.label: iv
children.text: The Bureau of Land Management.

children.label: v
children.text: The Corps of Engineers.

children.label: vi
children.text: The Bureau of Reclamation.

children.heading: .—Availability
children.label: 3 .—Availability
children.text: The inventories shall be made available to the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Updates
children.label: 4 .—Updates
children.text: The Secretaries of the appropriate Federal land management agencies shall update the inventories of the appropriate Federal land management agencies, as determined by the Secretary after collaborative discussions with the Secretaries of the appropriate Federal land management agencies.

children.heading: .—Review
children.label: 5 .—Review
children.text: A decision to add or remove a facility from the inventory shall not be considered a Federal action for purposes of review under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—Bicycle Safety
children.label: d .—Bicycle Safety
children.text: The Secretary of the appropriate Federal land management agency shall prohibit the use of bicycles on each federally owned road that has a speed limit of 30 miles per hour or greater and an adjacent paved path for use by bicycles within 100 yards of the road unless the Secretary determines that the bicycle level of service on that roadway is rated B or higher.

children.heading: .—Environmental document
children.label: A .—Environmental document
children.text: The term “environmental document” means an environmental impact statement, environmental assessment, categorical exclusion, or other document prepared under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: i
children.text: receives funds under this title; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is authorized under this section or section 204.

children.heading: .—Project sponsor
children.label: C .—Project sponsor
children.text: The term “project sponsor” means the Federal land management agency that seeks or receives funds under this title for a project.

children.heading: .—Environmental review to be completed by federal highway administration
children.label: 2 .—Environmental review to be completed by federal highway administration
A
children.text: requested by a project sponsor; and

children.label: B
children.text: all areas of analysis required by the project sponsor can be addressed.

children.heading: Federal land management agencies adoption of existing environmental review documents.—
children.label: 3 Federal land management agencies adoption of existing environmental review documents.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, if the Federal Highway Administration prepares an environmental document pursuant to paragraph (2), that environmental document shall address all areas of analysis required by a Federal land management agency.

children.heading: .—Independent evaluation
children.label: B .—Independent evaluation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a Federal land management agency shall not be required to conduct an independent evaluation to determine the adequacy of an environmental document prepared by the Federal Highway Administration pursuant to paragraph (2).

children.heading: .—Use of same document
children.label: C .—Use of same document
children.text: In authorizing or implementing a project, a Federal land management agency may use an environmental document previously prepared by the Federal Highway Administration for a project addressing the same or substantially the same action to the same extent that the Federal land management agency could adopt or use a document previously prepared by another Federal agency.

children.heading: .—Application by federal land management agencies of categorical exclusions established by federal highway administration
children.label: 4 .—Application by federal land management agencies of categorical exclusions established by federal highway administration
A
children.text: the project sponsor makes a determination, in consultation with the Federal Highway Administration, that the categorical exclusion applies to the project;

children.label: B
children.text: the project satisfies the conditions for a categorical exclusion under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: C
children.text: the use of the categorical exclusion does not otherwise conflict with the implementing regulations of the project sponsor, except any list of the project sponsor that designates categorical exclusions.

children.heading: .—Mitigation commitments
children.label: 5 .—Mitigation commitments
children.text: The Secretary shall assist the Federal land management agency with all design and mitigation commitments made jointly by the Secretary and the project sponsor in any environmental document prepared by the Secretary in accordance with this subsection.
